content:
Think of Writing Like an Iceberg
• The top of the iceberg = What the audience sees (your finished story).
• The huge mass underneath = All the unseen work (notes, research, backstory, drafts, alternate versions).

👉 The more you explore below the surface, the stronger the visible story will be.

1. Generating Story Ideas (From Logline to Expanded Narrative)

A logline is the shortest version of a story—usually a one-sentence summary that captures the core premise.

Example Loglines
1. A washed-up boxer gets a second chance at greatness but must choose between fame and his integrity.
2. A reclusive scientist discovers a portal to a parallel universe but risks erasing his own existence.
3. A woman wakes up with no memory and finds a note in her pocket that reads: “Don’t trust them.”
4. A struggling musician’s songs start predicting the future—but only tragic events.
5. A detective investigating a string of murders finds all the victims are alternate versions of himself.
